CVE-2018-17613.title: Telegram Desktop (aka tdesktop) 1.3.16 alpha, when "Use proxy" is ...
CVE-2018-17613.summary: Telegram Desktop (aka tdesktop) 1.3.16 alpha, when "Use proxy" is enabled, sends credentials and application data in cleartext over the SOCKS5 protocol.

CVE-2018-17780.title: Telegram Desktop (aka tdesktop) 1.3.14, and Telegram 3.3.0.0 WP8. ...
CVE-2018-17780.summary: Telegram Desktop (aka tdesktop) 1.3.14, and Telegram 3.3.0.0 WP8.1 on Windows, leaks end-user public and private IP addresses during a call because of an unsafe default behavior in which P2P connections are accepted from clients outside of the My Contacts list.

CVE-2020-12474.title: Telegram Desktop through 2.0.1, Telegram through 6.0.1 for Androi ...
CVE-2020-12474.summary: Telegram Desktop through 2.0.1, Telegram through 6.0.1 for Android, and Telegram through 6.0.1 for iOS allow an IDN Homograph attack via Punycode in a public URL or a group chat invitation URL.

CVE-2020-17448.title: Telegram Desktop through 2.1.13 allows a spoofed file type to byp ...
CVE-2020-17448.summary: Telegram Desktop through 2.1.13 allows a spoofed file type to bypass the Dangerous File Type Execution protection mechanism, as demonstrated by use of the chat window with a filename that lacks an extension.

CVE-2021-27351.title: The Terminate Session feature in the Telegram application through ...
CVE-2021-27351.summary: The Terminate Session feature in the Telegram application through 7.2.1 for Android, and through 2.4.7 for Windows and UNIX, fails to invalidate a recently active session.

CVE-2021-36769.title: A reordering issue exists in Telegram before 7.8.1 for Android, T ...
CVE-2021-36769.summary: A reordering issue exists in Telegram before 7.8.1 for Android, Telegram before 7.8.3 for iOS, and Telegram Desktop before 2.8.8. An attacker can cause the server to receive messages in a different order than they were sent a client.

CVE-2018-17780.title: Telegram Desktop (aka tdesktop) 1.3.14, and Telegram 3.3.0.0 WP8. ...
CVE-2018-17780.summary: Telegram Desktop (aka tdesktop) 1.3.14, and Telegram 3.3.0.0 WP8.1 on Windows, leaks end-user public and private IP addresses during a call because of an unsafe default behavior in which P2P connections are accepted from clients outside of the My Contacts list.
